notes: all combat numbers are simulator output on unverified constants
checkpoint levels are provisional (no level timeline in the match feed)
evidence deltas carry finished-inventory survivorship bias
the sim opens on Spectral Schematics, which the field rarely builds (0% pick) — a sim-only call; compare the meta cores below
the sim build reproduces only 33% of the field's core for Morigesh (it never builds Lifebinder, Megacosm) — some of those items ride mechanics the sim doesn't model yet

coachLine: Sim-optimal for 10s rotation vs squishies: first spike lands around minute 12 (Spectral Schematics), third item by minute 24. Take Demiurge: +12.2% on 20-second fights at minute 15.

all.name: Marrow
all.note: Marrow (Major): missing-health-dependent omnivamp; no sustain-uptime model yet

all.name: Nihil
all.note: Nihil (Major): accrual rate depends on combat uptime (how long Doom persists per fight), which the closed-form sim cannot observe

all.name: Aion
all.note: Aion (Major): economy effect; valuable but outside combat objectives (revisit with gold curves)

all.name: Exarch
all.note: Exarch (Major): buffs the HEAL TARGET (usually an ally) — team-side value outside the single-hero sim; self-heal uptime depends on heal cadence vs the 4s window

augments.name: Spirit Shred
augments.note: 5 stacks x 3.5%; ramp assumes one stack per second of basic attacks

augments.name: Ritual Of Death
augments.note: heals 70-90% of ult damage dealt (damage-coupled) and takedown cooldown refund; not yet modeled

metaBuilds.title: Magical Frontline
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for physical survivability, online around minute 23 — about 4,140 effective HP vs physical in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer agrees: within 2% of our best core on physical survivability. The winrate is earned, not luck.

metaBuilds.title: Magical Skirmisher
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for 10s rotation damage, online around minute 23 — about 2,150 damage over a 10s rotation in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees +19% more 10s rotation damage: try Spectral Schematics over Timewarp (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

metaBuilds.title: Magical Burst
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for one-combo burst, online around minute 24 — about 1,597 damage in one combo in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees +16% more one-combo burst: try Spectral Schematics over Combustion (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. The Fey deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Spectral Schematics: their 3s all-in loses 12% of its bite, at the cost of 27% of this kit's damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Argus deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Spectral Schematics: their 3s all-in loses 14% of its bite, at the cost of 25% of this kit's damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Renna's main threat is Mortal Coil; bait it before you commit, and mixed resist softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Spectral Schematics: their 3s all-in loses 14% of its bite, at the cost of 26% of this kit's damage.

matchups.gameplan: Your kill threat peaks early-to-mid: cash it in before their later items level the exchange. Iggy & Scorch deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Spectral Schematics: their 3s all-in loses 12% of its bite, at the cost of 27% of this kit's damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Gideon deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Spectral Schematics: their 3s all-in loses 14% of its bite, at the cost of 25% of this kit's damage.

matchups.gameplan: Even kill threat throughout: this lane is decided by mechanics and wave control, not scaling. Countess deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Spectral Schematics: their 3s all-in loses 11% of its bite, at the cost of 28% of this kit's damage.

laneSteer.provenance: “Cursed Infestation” ⇒ ability-burst; the sim models this augment — field midlane 50.7% over 1,212 games.
